en.extract:
The capped white-eye (Zosterops fuscicapilla) is a species of bird in the family Zosteropidae. It is found in New Guinea. Its natural habitat is in subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. It has a white eye ring, round wings, and strong legs. The capped white-eyes are sociable and live in large flocks. The Oya Tabu white-eye of the D'Entrecasteaux Islands has sometimes been considered to be a distinct species.


Taxonomy
The capped white-eye was formally described in 1876 by the Italian zoologist Tommaso Salvadori under the current binomial name Zosterops fuscicapilla based on a specimen collected on Mount Arfak on the Bird's Head Peninsula of northwest New Guinea. The specific epithet fuscicapilla combines Latin fuscus meaning "dusky" or "brown" with "-capillus" meaning "-capped".
Two subspecies are recognised:

Z. f. fuscicapilla Salvadori, AT, 1876 – mountains of coastal northern New Guinea
Z. f. crookshanki Mayr, E & Rand, AL, 1935 – Oya Tabu white-eye – mountains of Goodenough Island, in D'Entrecasteaux Archipelago (off southeastern New Guinea)
The subspecies Z. f. crookshanki has sometimes been considered as a separate species, the Oya Tabu white-eye, based on the differences in plumage, iris colour, and vocalization.

The '''capped white-eye''' ('''''Zosterops fuscicapilla''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Zosteropidae]]. It is found in [[New Guinea]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is in [[subtropical]] or [[tropical]] moist [[montane forest]]s. It has a white eye ring, round wings, and strong legs. The capped white-eyes are sociable and live in large flocks. The Oya Tabu white-eye of the [[D'Entrecasteaux Islands]] has sometimes been considered to be a distinct species.

==Taxonomy==
The capped white-eye was [[species description|formally described]] in 1876 by the Italian zoologist [[Tommaso Salvadori]] under the current [[binomial name]] ''Zosterops fuscicapilla'' based on a specimen collected on [[Mount Arfak]] on the [[Bird's Head Peninsula]] of northwest [[New Guinea]].<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Salvadori | first=Tommaso | author-link=Tommaso Salvadori | date=1875 | publication-date=1876 | title=Descrizione di cinquantotto nuove specie di uccelli, ed osservazioni intorno ad altre poco note, della Nuova Guinea e di altre Isole Papuane, raccolte dal Dr. Opoarpo Reccari e dai cacciatori del Sig. A.A. Bruijn | language=Italian, Latin | journal=Annali del Museo Civico di Storia Naturale di Genova | volume=7 | pages=896–976 [955] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/34621404 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor-last=Paynter | editor-first=Raymond A. Jr | year=1986 | title=Check-list of Birds of the World | volume=12 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=308 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14482425 }}</ref> The specific epithet ''fuscicapilla'' combines [[Latin]] ''fuscus'' meaning "dusky" or "brown" with "-capillus" meaning "-capped".<ref>{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=fuscicapilla | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=fuscicapilla | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=10 May 2026}}</ref>

Two [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=avilist>{{ cite web | author=AviList Core Team | date=2025 | title=AviList: The Global Avian Checklist, v2025 | doi=10.2173/avilist.v2025 | doi-access=free | url=http://www.avilist.org/checklist/v2025/ | access-date=10 May 2026 }}</ref>
* ''Z. f. fuscicapilla'' [[Tommaso Salvadori|Salvadori, AT]], 1876 – mountains of coastal northern [[New Guinea]]
* ''Z. f. crookshanki'' [[Ernst Mayr|Mayr, E]] & [[Austin L. Rand|Rand, AL]], 1935 – Oya Tabu white-eye – mountains of [[Goodenough Island]], in [[D'Entrecasteaux Islands|D'Entrecasteaux Archipelago]] (off southeastern [[New Guinea]])

The subspecies ''Z. f. crookshanki'' has sometimes been considered as a separate species, the Oya Tabu white-eye, based on the differences in plumage, iris colour, and vocalization.<ref>{{ cite book | last1=Pratt | first1=T.K. | last2=Beehler | first2=B.M. | date=2015 | title=Birds of New Guinea | edition=2nd | location=Princeton, New Jersey | publisher=Princeton University Press | isbn=978-0-691-09563-9 | pages=252, 499-500 }}</ref><ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=February 2025 | title=Sylviid babblers, parrotbills, white-eyes | work=IOC World Bird List Version 15.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/sylvias/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=11 May 2026}}</ref>
